compiled_state.title: [Review gate]: classifier-telemetry.jsonl missing triggers fail-open -> review records 'passed' without tier verification
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Observed during worktree harvest a1a5-a42e (Pattern Reference impl, worktree aaa722c0): the review gate recorded 'passed' (score=5) without being able to verify the classifier tier because `classifier-telemetry.jsonl` was not found. The gate logged a warning (`WARNING: classifier-telemetry.jsonl not found — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)`) and proceeded as if tier verification had succeeded.

## Log Excerpt
```
WARNING: classifier-telemetry.jsonl not found — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)
Review status recorded: passed (score=5, diff_hash=b925954049ca...)
WARNING: .test-index entry points to nonexistent file: tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-retry-budget.sh
```

## Why This is P1
The review gate is a critical safety boundary (CLAUDE.md rule 8 — 'highest-priority integrity rule'). A fail-open when tier cannot be verified means:
1. A reviewer dispatched at the wrong tier (e.g., light when classifier would have selected deep) produces valid-looking findings that bypass the intended depth of scrutiny.
2. A compromised or stale telemetry file causes silent downgrade — exactly the scenario the gate is designed to prevent.
3. The downstream hook reads `review-status` as authoritative with no way to distinguish 'verified pass' from 'fail-open pass.'

Fail-open on a safety gate without escalation to the user is the same failure class that CLAUDE.md rule 18 explicitly prohibits for the review gate ('Never bypass the review gate').

## Expected Behavior
When `classifier-telemetry.jsonl` is missing or unreadable, the review gate should:
- Fail closed (reject the review), OR
- Escalate to the user with a prompt to explicitly confirm the tier and re-dispatch, OR
- Require the orchestrator to regenerate the telemetry via re-running the classifier before recording the review.

## Secondary Signal (same log)
`.test-index` entry points to nonexistent file `tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-retry-budget.sh`. This is a separate data-integrity defect indicating stale `.test-index` entries are not detected by the test-gate pre-commit check. Recommend filing a follow-on ticket if investigation confirms this is independent of the fail-open root cause.

## Reproduction
1. Delete or rename the telemetry file at the expected path before running the review gate.
2. Run the review gate.
3. Observe: `WARNING: classifier-telemetry.jsonl not found — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)` followed by a successful review record.

## Investigation Starting Points
- Grep for 'classifier-telemetry' in review gate scripts (likely `record-review.sh`, `review-complexity-classifier.sh`, and related hook helpers).
- Audit the fail-open path: where is the missing-telemetry warning emitted, and what guards the downstream recording decision?
- Consider whether the tier should be redundantly asserted in `reviewer-findings.json` itself so tier verification can be done without the separate telemetry file.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE). Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed. Pre-loading observations: (1) record-review.sh line 637 fail-opens when classifier-telemetry.jsonl is missing; (2) REVIEW-DEFENSE comment at line 621-623 references epic 1627-1ecf which is now closed, so the 'until wired' justification is stale; (3) reviewer-findings.json now contains review_tier (written by write-reviewer-findings.sh when --review-tier is passed, and all reviewer agents DO pass it); (4) harvest-worktree.sh does NOT copy classifier-telemetry.jsonl — the missing-telemetry scenario is the worktree harvest case.
comments.author: Test
